title: Bitcoin Stamps Database
description: Rules for understanding and working with the Bitcoin Stamps database schema and operations.

patterns.description: Comprehensive database schema and operation guidelines
instructions: This is the authoritative source for database structure and usage.
Includes table definitions, relationships, and common operations.
Reference this document before making any database changes.
Use parameterized queries with %s placeholders to prevent SQL injection.
Follow connection patterns to ensure proper connection management.
Always close connections to return them to the pool.

patterns.description: SQL schema definition for all tables
instructions: Complete SQL schema definition for the database.
Reference for table structure and indexes.
Make sure to update this file when modifying database schema.
Follow the same connection management patterns as in database.py.
Ensure consistent error handling and transaction management.

conceptDefinitions.definition: Technique for efficient database connection management where connections are reused rather than created for each operation.
Using placeholders (%s) in SQL queries and passing parameters separately to prevent SQL injection.
Group of database operations that are treated as a single unit, ensuring consistency through commit or rollback.
Main table for storing stamp data including transaction hash, creator, and metadata.
Table for storing SRC-20 token operations (deploy, mint, transfer).
Table for storing validated SRC-20 token operations.
Table for tracking current SRC-20 token balances by address.
Table for storing SRC-101 domain operations.

relatedDocumentation.description: System architecture including database component relationships
Protocol specifications that inform database structure
